methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

private.name: Matic Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Daylight Computer is a Series A-stage consumer technology company founded by Anjan Katta that has raised $10M to date. The comparable universe is limited, consisting of one identified private peer (Matic Robotics) and no public comparables, reflecting the nascent and fragmented nature of the frontier-tech consumer segment.

The valuation analysis applies stage-progression methodology, which is applicable given the company's defined funding milestone. Using total raised ($10M) as a proxy base value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied value of $20M. This single-method triangulation is constrained by limited comparable data and the absence of revenue or profitability metrics to support alternative approaches such as revenue multiples or DCF analysis.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M (low) to $23M (high), with a median of $20M. The narrow 35% spread between low and high reflects the mechanical nature of the stage-progression method rather than deep market validation. Key uncertainties include the company's actual revenue trajectory, competitive positioning relative to Matic Robotics and other unlisted peers, and whether the 2.0x multiplier accurately reflects investor appetite for consumer frontier-tech at Series A expansion. Additional comparables and operational metrics would strengthen conviction in this range.
